chunk_summary: This section outlines macronutrient distribution targets for children using Dietary Reference Intakes (DRI), emphasizing age-specific ranges, cultural adaptations, and clinical considerations for growth, obesity, and special conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand the Acceptable Macronutrient Distribution Range (AMDR) for children aged 1–18 years.
Learn how to adapt macronutrient targets to cultural dietary practices, such as using visible fats in Indian diets.
Recognize clinical scenarios requiring specialist-led macronutrient adjustments, such as fat malabsorption or severe feeding limitations.
key_facts: For ages 1–3 years: fat 30–40%, carbohydrate 45–65%, protein 5–20% of energy.
For ages 4–18 years: fat 25–35%, carbohydrate 45–65%, protein 10–30% of energy.
In wasting, increase energy density by adding visible fats to staple foods without increasing meal volume.
In obesity with dyslipidemia risk, keep saturated fat ≤10% of energy and avoid industrial trans fats.
Macronutrient distribution should align with cultural food patterns and physiologic needs.
reference_quote: Use the DRI Acceptable Macronutrient Distribution Range (AMDR) as a bedside framework, not as a rigid diet.
document_key_topics: Clinical considerations for growth and obesity
